Yes, I will be doing a Conversation with him. He is a [Professor of History at Northwestern](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=ccf687d0442a709190d9356905bc7ab2c15faee9cbf748dbe3deaf2c53618c32&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92365&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=b2f4aa65f5c256bbca54abb9866c8c40&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9oaXN0b3J5Lm5vcnRod2VzdGVybi5lZHUvcGVvcGxlL2ZhY3VsdHkvY29yZS1mYWN1bHR5L3BhdWwtZ2lsbGluZ2hhbS5odG1s&email_id=787ab0c24e42ceff186b8fd7b6f497e3), specializing in Mexico and to some extent the Caribbean. He has translated [a Mexican book on Edgar Allan Poe](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=c0666e148a7e9d49c4404e81da6d92b0a8047a31f08ddddf9cb1435736080236&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92365&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=338bcb9edde0d345f5cc48ceda7c66c5&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=787ab0c24e42ceff186b8fd7b6f497e3). I am learning a good deal from his new 700 pp. book [Mexico: A 500-Year History](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=695aa1780dbb4cdb77ce2fc8ac50e12276e34dd0751eb69fdda9ecfc52745a9a&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92365&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=68a6f956653ca2f612ff22cdddeb5780&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=787ab0c24e42ceff186b8fd7b6f497e3), and I very much like his earlier work on Mexico and violence. Here is [an NYT review of the new book](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=ff6ec30c47ea3d2275194d4bc77acc8b4a8a39929fa60f8f43670b8130984b44&blog_id=42693868&post_id=92365&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=00dae00bd443cb724b3f4c85a864c797&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubnl0aW1lcy5jb20vMjAyNS8xMS8xOS9ib29rcy9yZXZpZXcvbWV4aWNvLXBhdWwtZ2lsbGluZ2hhbS5odG1s&email_id=787ab0c24e42ceff186b8fd7b6f497e3).
So what should I ask him?
